description: Create an OpenClaw skill that combines vercel-labs/json-render + scoutos/atoms + onhyper.io to generate full SPAs

steps.title: Research onhyper.io deployment API
steps.description: Understand how to deploy to onhyper.io - API endpoints, auth, file upload process, proxy configuration
successCriteria: Documented deployment API endpoints
Understood auth mechanism (API key flow)
Identified proxy configuration for Scout API routing
Found or confirmed single HTML deployment support

endpoints.create: POST /api/apps - {name, slug, description, html, css, js}

endpoints.list: GET /api/secrets - List stored API keys (preview only)

proxy.routingNotes: Include X-App-Slug header for routing and rate limiting

findings.notesForScoutIntegration: Use /proxy/scout-atoms/* endpoint to route Scout OS API calls through onhyper. Store Scout API key as 'scout-atoms' secret. Apps can then call Scout without exposing keys client-side.

steps.title: Create Vite React template with single HTML output
steps.description: Set up a minimal Vite + React + TypeScript project that builds to a single inlined HTML file
successCriteria: Vite project scaffolded with React + TypeScript
Vite config produces single index.html with all assets inlined
Build output verified: dist/index.html only (no separate JS/CSS files)
Dev server works for local testing
steps.notes: Used vite-plugin-singlefile for inlining. Build outputs single 194KB index.html with all CSS/JS inlined. Dev server tested on port 5173.

steps.title: Integrate json-render into Vite template
steps.description: Add @json-render/core and @json-render/react packages, create a basic catalog with 3-5 components
successCriteria: json-render packages installed and configured
Component catalog defined (Card, Metric, Button minimum)
React Renderer working with test spec
Streaming support verified (SpecStream)
steps.notes: Installed @json-render/core@0.6.1 and @json-render/react@0.6.1. Created Zod-based catalog with Card, Metric, Button components. Used defineRegistry + Renderer pattern. Test spec renders correctly. Build produces 297KB index.html (90KB gzipped). SpecStream support is available via useUIStream hook.

steps.title: Create Scout Atoms schema layer
steps.description: Build the integration between json-render component props and Scout atom schemas
successCriteria: Type-safe schema definitions (TypeScript types from atom schemas)
Schema-to-catalog mapping documented
Test with demo12 Scout API key
At least 2 atom types working (e.g., Contact, Task)

steps.title: Build proxy layer for onhyper endpoints
steps.description: Create the proxy configuration that routes frontend API calls through onhyper to Scout
successCriteria: Proxy configuration defined (routes map)
Frontend API calls abstracted behind proxy layer
No hardcoded API keys in frontend bundle
Test proxy routing with mock responses

steps.title: Create OpenClaw skill package
steps.description: Package everything as an OpenClaw skill with SKILL.md and commands
successCriteria: SKILL.md documented with all commands
Skill installed in ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/spa-builder/
spa-scaffold CLI command working
Template generation creates valid project structure

steps.title: End-to-end demo deployment
steps.description: Deploy a complete demo SPA to onhyper.io using the skill
successCriteria: Demo app created (e.g., task list or contacts dashboard)
Successfully built to single HTML
Deployed to onhyper.io
Live URL working with Scout backend integration
